name: Basic-Science Viva — Speaking About Foundational Medicine
_note: Oral exam on basic science for pre-clinical IMGs. Scores ENGLISH and the ability to EXPLAIN concepts aloud, not exhaustive factual mastery. Standard 5 metric keys kept so scores/DB/self-assessment keep working; labels re-pitched. medical_accuracy = correctness of the basic-science content; clinical_reasoning slot = clarity/structure of the spoken explanation.

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear
anchors.5-6: Some errors in tense/articles/word order, but understandable
anchors.3-4: Frequent errors; examiner must work to follow

anchors.9-10: Concepts explained correctly with the right terms; misconceptions absent
anchors.5-6: Core idea right but with gaps or loose terminology
anchors.3-4: Several factual errors or confused concepts

anchors.9-10: Explanations are logically ordered and easy to follow; defines terms before using them
anchors.3-4: Hard to follow; ideas out of order

anchors.9-10: Medical terms pronounced clearly; smooth, confident delivery
anchors.7-8: Mostly clear; occasional hesitation or mispronunciation
anchors.5-6: Understandable but effortful; several terms mispronounced
anchors.3-4: Frequent long pauses or unclear terms

anchors.9-10: Calm, engaged, asks for clarification appropriately; handles not-knowing gracefully

checklist.item: answered the examiner's question rather than avoiding it
used at least some correct anatomical/physiological terminology in English
explained a concept in a logical order (not just a list of words)
asked the examiner to repeat or clarify when unsure (instead of guessing silently)
spoke in full sentences rather than single words
